      92              : /*
      93              :  * Default policy for whether to apply RECOVER_RELATION_BUILD_MEMORY:

     152              : 
     153              : /*
     154              :  * in_progress_list is a stack of ongoing RelationBuildDesc() calls.  CREATE
     155              :  * INDEX CONCURRENTLY makes catalog changes under ShareUpdateExclusiveLock.
     156              :  * It critically relies on each backend absorbing those changes no later than
     157              :  * next transaction start.  Hence, RelationBuildDesc() loops until it finishes
     158              :  * without accepting a relevant invalidation.  (Most invalidation consumers
     159              :  * don't do this.)
     160              :  */
     161              : typedef struct inprogressent
     162              : {
     163              :         Oid                     reloid;                 /* OID of relation being built */
     164              :         bool            invalidated;    /* whether an invalidation arrived for it */
     165              : } InProgressEnt;
     166              : 
     167              : static InProgressEnt *in_progress_list;
     168              : static int      in_progress_list_len;
     169              : static int      in_progress_list_maxlen;
     170              : 
     171              : /*
     172              :  * eoxact_list[] stores the OIDs of relations that (might) need AtEOXact
     173              :  * cleanup work.  This list intentionally has limited size; if it overflows,
     174              :  * we fall back to scanning the whole hashtable.  There is no value in a very
     175              :  * large list because (1) at some point, a hash_seq_search scan is faster than
     176              :  * retail lookups, and (2) the value of this is to reduce EOXact work for
     177              :  * short transactions, which can't have dirtied all that many tables anyway.
     178              :  * EOXactListAdd() does not bother to prevent duplicate list entries, so the
     179              :  * cleanup processing must be idempotent.
     180              :  */
     181              : #define MAX_EOXACT_LIST 32
     182              : static Oid      eoxact_list[MAX_EOXACT_LIST];
     183              : static int      eoxact_list_len = 0;
     184              : static bool eoxact_list_overflowed = false;
     185              : 
     186              : #define EOXactListAdd(rel) \
     187              :         do { \
     188              :                 if (eoxact_list_len < MAX_EOXACT_LIST) \
     189              :                         eoxact_list[eoxact_list_len++] = (rel)->rd_id; \
     190              :                 else \
     191              :                         eoxact_list_overflowed = true; \
     192              :         } while (0)
     193              : 
     194              : /*
     195              :  * EOXactTupleDescArray stores TupleDescs that (might) need AtEOXact
     196              :  * cleanup work.  The array expands as needed; there is no hashtable because
     197              :  * we don't need to access individual items except at EOXact.
     198              :  */
     199              : static TupleDesc *EOXactTupleDescArray;
     200              : static int      NextEOXactTupleDescNum = 0;
     201              : static int      EOXactTupleDescArrayLen = 0;
     202              :

     211              :                                                                                    HASH_ENTER, &found); \
     212              :         if (found) \
     213              :         { \

     216              :                 Assert(replace_allowed); \
     217              :                 hentry->reldesc = (RELATION); \
     218              :                 if (RelationHasReferenceCountZero(_old_rel)) \

     223              :         } \
     224              :         else \
     225              :                 hentry->reldesc = (RELATION); \
     226              : } while(0)

     317              : 
     318              : /*
     319              :  *              ScanPgRelation
     320              :  *
     321              :  *              This is used by RelationBuildDesc to find a pg_class
     322              :  *              tuple matching targetRelId.  The caller must hold at least
     323              :  *              AccessShareLock on the target relid to prevent concurrent-update
     324              :  *              scenarios; it isn't guaranteed that all scans used to build the
     325              :  *              relcache entry will use the same snapshot.  If, for example,
     326              :  *              an attribute were to be added after scanning pg_class and before
     327              :  *              scanning pg_attribute, relnatts wouldn't match.
     328              :  *
     329              :  *              NB: the returned tuple has been copied into palloc'd storage
     330              :  *              and must eventually be freed with heap_freetuple.
     331              :  */
     332              : static HeapTuple
     333         3292 : ScanPgRelation(Oid targetRelId, bool indexOK, bool force_non_historic)
     334              : {
     335              :         HeapTuple       pg_class_tuple;
     336              :         Relation        pg_class_desc;
     337              :         SysScanDesc pg_class_scan;
     338              :         ScanKeyData key[1];
     339         3292 :         Snapshot        snapshot = NULL;
     340              : 
     341              :         /*
     342              :          * If something goes wrong during backend startup, we might find ourselves
     343              :          * trying to read pg_class before we've selected a database.  That ain't
     344              :          * gonna work, so bail out with a useful error message.  If this happens,
     345              :          * it probably means a relcache entry that needs to be nailed isn't.
     346              :          */
     347         3292 :         if (!OidIsValid(MyDatabaseId))
     348            0 :                 elog(FATAL, "cannot read pg_class without having selected a database");
     349              : 
     350              :         /*
     351              :          * form a scan key
     352              :          */
     353         3292 :         ScanKeyInit(&key[0],
     354              :                                 Anum_pg_class_oid,
     355              :                                 BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
     356              :                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(targetRelId));
     357              : 
     358              :         /*
     359              :          * Open pg_class and fetch a tuple.  Force heap scan if we haven't yet
     360              :          * built the critical relcache entries (this includes initdb and startup
     361              :          * without a pg_internal.init file).  The caller can also force a heap
     362              :          * scan by setting indexOK == false.
     363              :          */
     364         3292 :         pg_class_desc = table_open(RelationRelationId, AccessShareLock);
     365              : 
     366              :         /*
     367              :          * The caller might need a tuple that's newer than the one the historic
     368              :          * snapshot; currently the only case requiring to do so is looking up the
     369              :          * relfilenode of non mapped system relations during decoding. That
     370              :          * snapshot can't change in the midst of a relcache build, so there's no
     371              :          * need to register the snapshot.
     372              :          */
     373         3292 :         if (force_non_historic)
     374            0 :                 snapshot = GetNonHistoricCatalogSnapshot(RelationRelationId);
     375              : 
     376         3292 :         pg_class_scan = systable_beginscan(pg_class_desc, ClassOidIndexId,
     377         3292 :                                                                            indexOK && criticalRelcachesBuilt,
     378              :                                                                            snapshot,
     379              :                                                                            1, key);
     380              : 
     381         3292 :         pg_class_tuple = systable_getnext(pg_class_scan);
     382              : 
     383              :         /*
     384              :          * Must copy tuple before releasing buffer.
     385              :          */
     386         3292 :         if (HeapTupleIsValid(pg_class_tuple))
     387         3292 :                 pg_class_tuple = heap_copytuple(pg_class_tuple);
     388              : 
     389              :         /* all done */
     390         3292 :         systable_endscan(pg_class_scan);
     391         3292 :         table_close(pg_class_desc, AccessShareLock);
     392              : 
     393         3292 :         return pg_class_tuple;
     394              : }
     395              : 
     396              : /*
     397              :  *              AllocateRelationDesc
     398              :  *
     399              :  *              This is used to allocate memory for a new relation descriptor
     400              :  *              and initialize the rd_rel field from the given pg_class tuple.
     401              :  */
     402              : static Relation
     403         3110 : AllocateRelationDesc(Form_pg_class relp)
     404              : {
     405              :         Relation        relation;
     406              :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
     407              :         Form_pg_class relationForm;
     408              : 
     409              :         /* Relcache entries must live in CacheMemoryContext */
     410         3110 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
     411              : 
     412              :         /*
     413              :          * allocate and zero space for new relation descriptor
     414              :          */
     415         3110 :         relation = (Relation) palloc0(sizeof(RelationData));
     416              : 
     417              :         /* make sure relation is marked as having no open file yet */
     418         3110 :         relation->rd_smgr = NULL;
     419              : 
     420              :         /*
     421              :          * Copy the relation tuple form
     422              :          *
     423              :          * We only allocate space for the fixed fields, ie, CLASS_TUPLE_SIZE. The
     424              :          * variable-length fields (relacl, reloptions) are NOT stored in the
     425              :          * relcache --- there'd be little point in it, since we don't copy the
     426              :          * tuple's nulls bitmap and hence wouldn't know if the values are valid.
     427              :          * Bottom line is that relacl *cannot* be retrieved from the relcache. Get
     428              :          * it from the syscache if you need it.  The same goes for the original
     429              :          * form of reloptions (however, we do store the parsed form of reloptions
     430              :          * in rd_options).
     431              :          */
     432         3110 :         relationForm = (Form_pg_class) palloc(CLASS_TUPLE_SIZE);
     433              : 
     434         3110 :         memcpy(relationForm, relp, CLASS_TUPLE_SIZE);
     435              : 
     436              :         /* initialize relation tuple form */
     437         3110 :         relation->rd_rel = relationForm;
     438              : 
     439              :         /* and allocate attribute tuple form storage */
     440         3110 :         relation->rd_att = CreateTemplateTupleDesc(relationForm->relnatts);
     441              :         /* which we mark as a reference-counted tupdesc */
     442         3110 :         relation->rd_att->tdrefcount = 1;
     443              : 
     444         3110 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
     445              : 
     446         3110 :         return relation;
     447              : }
     448              : 
     449              : /*
     450              :  * RelationParseRelOptions
     451              :  *              Convert pg_class.reloptions into pre-parsed rd_options
     452              :  *
     453              :  * tuple is the real pg_class tuple (not rd_rel!) for relation
     454              :  *
     455              :  * Note: rd_rel and (if an index) rd_indam must be valid already
     456              :  */
     457              : static void
     458         3291 : RelationParseRelOptions(Relation relation, HeapTuple tuple)
     459              : {
     460              :         bytea      *options;
     461              :         amoptions_function amoptsfn;
     462              : 
     463         3291 :         relation->rd_options = NULL;
     464              : 
     465              :         /*
     466              :          * Look up any AM-specific parse function; fall out if relkind should not
     467              :          * have options.
     468              :          */
     469         3291 :         switch (relation->rd_rel->relkind)
     470              :         {
     471         2479 :                 case RELKIND_RELATION:
     472              :                 case RELKIND_TOASTVALUE:
     473              :                 case RELKIND_VIEW:
     474              :                 case RELKIND_MATVIEW:
     475              :                 case RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE:
     476         2479 :                         amoptsfn = NULL;
     477         2479 :                         break;
     478          808 :                 case RELKIND_INDEX:
     479              :                 case RELKIND_PARTITIONED_INDEX:
     480          808 :                         amoptsfn = relation->rd_indam->amoptions;
     481          808 :                         break;
     482            4 :                 default:
     483            4 :                         return;
     484              :         }
     485              : 
     486              :         /*
     487              :          * Fetch reloptions from tuple; have to use a hardwired descriptor because
     488              :          * we might not have any other for pg_class yet (consider executing this
     489              :          * code for pg_class itself)
     490              :          */
     491         3287 :         options = extractRelOptions(tuple, GetPgClassDescriptor(), amoptsfn);
     492              : 
     493              :         /*
     494              :          * Copy parsed data into CacheMemoryContext.  To guard against the

     496              :          * parsing in the caller's memory context and copy the results into

     498              :          */
     499         3287 :         if (options)

     753              : /*
     754              :  *              RelationBuildRuleLock
     755              :  *
     756              :  *              Form the relation's rewrite rules from information in
     757              :  *              the pg_rewrite system catalog.
     758              :  *
     759              :  * Note: The rule parsetrees are potentially very complex node structures.
     760              :  * To allow these trees to be freed when the relcache entry is flushed,
     761              :  * we make a private memory context to hold the RuleLock information for
     762              :  * each relcache entry that has associated rules.  The context is used
     763              :  * just for rule info, not for any other subsidiary data of the relcache
     764              :  * entry, because that keeps the update logic in RelationClearRelation()
     765              :  * manageable.  The other subsidiary data structures are simple enough

     767              :  */

     792              : 
     793              :         /*

     797          290 :         maxlocks = 4;

     801              : 
     802              :         /*
     803              :          * form a scan key

     870              :                 /*
     871              :                  * We want the rule's table references to be checked as though by the

     874              :                  * rtable entries.  We have to look at the qual as well, in case it

     878              :                  * it is stored, is that otherwise ALTER TABLE OWNER would have to

     891              :                 }
     892          270 :                 rules[numlocks++] = rule;
     893              :         }

    1015              : /*
    1016              :  *              equalRSDesc
    1017              :  *
    1018              :  *              Determine whether two RowSecurityDesc's are equivalent

    1020              : static bool
    1021         1511 : equalRSDesc(RowSecurityDesc *rsdesc1, RowSecurityDesc *rsdesc2)
    1022              : {
    1023              :         ListCell   *lc,
    1024              :                            *rc;
    1025              : 
    1026         1511 :         if (rsdesc1 == NULL && rsdesc2 == NULL)

    1035              :

    1049              : /*

    1595              : /*
    1596              :  * IndexSupportInitialize
    1597              :  *              Initializes an index's cached opclass information,
    1598              :  *              given the index's pg_index.indclass entry.
    1599              :  *
    1600              :  * Data is returned into *indexSupport, *opFamily, and *opcInType,
    1601              :  * which are arrays allocated by the caller.
    1602              :  *
    1603              :  * The caller also passes maxSupportNumber and maxAttributeNumber, since these
    1604              :  * indicate the size of the arrays it has allocated --- but in practice these
    1605              :  * numbers must always match those obtainable from the system catalog entries
    1606              :  * for the index and access method.
    1607              :  */
    1608              : static void
    1609          799 : IndexSupportInitialize(oidvector *indclass,
    1610              :                                            RegProcedure *indexSupport,
    1611              :                                            Oid *opFamily,
    1612              :                                            Oid *opcInType,
    1613              :                                            StrategyNumber maxSupportNumber,
    1614              :                                            AttrNumber maxAttributeNumber)
    1615              : {
    1616              :         int                     attIndex;
    1617              : 
    1618         2071 :         for (attIndex = 0; attIndex < maxAttributeNumber; attIndex++)
    1619              :         {
    1620              :                 OpClassCacheEnt *opcentry;
    1621              : 
    1622         1272 :                 if (!OidIsValid(indclass->values[attIndex]))
    1623            0 :                         elog(ERROR, "bogus pg_index tuple");
    1624              : 
    1625              :                 /* look up the info for this opclass, using a cache */
    1626         1272 :                 opcentry = LookupOpclassInfo(indclass->values[attIndex],
    1627              :                                                                          maxSupportNumber);
    1628              : 
    1629              :                 /* copy cached data into relcache entry */
    1630         1272 :                 opFamily[attIndex] = opcentry->opcfamily;
    1631         1272 :                 opcInType[attIndex] = opcentry->opcintype;

    1638              : 
    1639              : /*
    1640              :  * LookupOpclassInfo
    1641              :  *
    1642              :  * This routine maintains a per-opclass cache of the information needed
    1643              :  * by IndexSupportInitialize().  This is more efficient than relying on
    1644              :  * the catalog cache, because we can load all the info about a particular
    1645              :  * opclass in a single indexscan of pg_amproc.
    1646              :  *
    1647              :  * The information from pg_am about expected range of support function
    1648              :  * numbers is passed in, rather than being looked up, mainly because the
    1649              :  * caller will have it already.
    1650              :  *
    1651              :  * Note there is no provision for flushing the cache.  This is OK at the
    1652              :  * moment because there is no way to ALTER any interesting properties of an
    1653              :  * existing opclass --- all you can do is drop it, which will result in
    1654              :  * a useless but harmless dead entry in the cache.  To support altering
    1655              :  * opclass membership (not the same as opfamily membership!), we'd need to
    1656              :  * be able to flush this cache as well as the contents of relcache entries
    1657              :  * for indexes.
    1658              :  */
    1659              : static OpClassCacheEnt *
    1660         1272 : LookupOpclassInfo(Oid operatorClassOid,
    1661              :                                   StrategyNumber numSupport)
    1662              : {
    1663              :         OpClassCacheEnt *opcentry;
    1664              :         bool            found;
    1665              :         Relation        rel;
    1666              :         SysScanDesc scan;
    1667              :         ScanKeyData skey[3];
    1668              :         HeapTuple       htup;
    1669              :         bool            indexOK;
    1670              : 
    1671         1272 :         if (OpClassCache == NULL)
    1672              :         {
    1673              :                 /* First time through: initialize the opclass cache */
    1674              :                 HASHCTL         ctl;
    1675              : 
    1676              :                 /* Also make sure CacheMemoryContext exists */
    1677            6 :                 if (!CacheMemoryContext)
    1678            0 :                         CreateCacheMemoryContext();
    1679              : 
    1680           84 :                 MemSet(&ctl, 0, sizeof(ctl));
    1681            6 :                 ctl.keysize = sizeof(Oid);
    1682            6 :                 ctl.entrysize = sizeof(OpClassCacheEnt);
    1683            6 :                 OpClassCache = hash_create("Operator class cache", 64,
    1684              :                                                                    &ctl, HASH_ELEM | HASH_BLOBS);
    1685              :         }
    1686              : 
    1687         1272 :         opcentry = (OpClassCacheEnt *) hash_search(OpClassCache,
    1688              :                                                                                            (void *) &operatorClassOid,
    1689              :                                                                                            HASH_ENTER, &found);
    1690              : 
    1691         1272 :         if (!found)
    1692              :         {
    1693              :                 /* Initialize new entry */
    1694           39 :                 opcentry->valid = false;     /* until known OK */
    1695           39 :                 opcentry->numSupport = numSupport;
    1696           39 :                 opcentry->supportProcs = NULL;       /* filled below */
    1697              :         }
    1698              :         else
    1699              :         {
    1700              :                 Assert(numSupport == opcentry->numSupport);
    1701              :         }
    1702              : 
    1703              :         /*
    1704              :          * When aggressively testing cache-flush hazards, we disable the operator
    1705              :          * class cache and force reloading of the info on each call.  This models
    1706              :          * no real-world behavior, since the cache entries are never invalidated
    1707              :          * otherwise.  However it can be helpful for detecting bugs in the cache
    1708              :          * loading logic itself, such as reliance on a non-nailed index.  Given
    1709              :          * the limited use-case and the fact that this adds a great deal of
    1710              :          * expense, we enable it only in CLOBBER_CACHE_RECURSIVELY mode.
    1711              :          */
    1712              : #if defined(CLOBBER_CACHE_RECURSIVELY)
    1713              :         opcentry->valid = false;
    1714              : #endif
    1715              : 
    1716         1272 :         if (opcentry->valid)
    1717         1233 :                 return opcentry;
    1718              : 
    1719              :         /*
    1720              :          * Need to fill in new entry.  First allocate space, unless we already did
    1721              :          * so in some previous attempt.
    1722              :          */
    1723           39 :         if (opcentry->supportProcs == NULL && numSupport > 0)
    1724           39 :                 opcentry->supportProcs = (RegProcedure *)
    1725           39 :                         MemoryContextAllocZero(CacheMemoryContext,
    1726              :                                                                    numSupport * sizeof(RegProcedure));
    1727              : 
    1728              :         /*
    1729              :          * To avoid infinite recursion during startup, force heap scans if we're
    1730              :          * looking up info for the opclasses used by the indexes we would like to
    1731              :          * reference here.
    1732              :          */
    1733           57 :         indexOK = criticalRelcachesBuilt ||
    1734           18 :                 (operatorClassOid != OID_BTREE_OPS_OID &&
    1735           14 :                  operatorClassOid != INT2_BTREE_OPS_OID);
    1736              : 
    1737              :         /*
    1738              :          * We have to fetch the pg_opclass row to determine its opfamily and
    1739              :          * opcintype, which are needed to look up related operators and functions.
    1740              :          * It'd be convenient to use the syscache here, but that probably doesn't
    1741              :          * work while bootstrapping.
    1742              :          */
    1743           39 :         ScanKeyInit(&skey[0],
    1744              :                                 Anum_pg_opclass_oid,
    1745              :                                 BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
    1746              :                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(operatorClassOid));
    1747           39 :         rel = table_open(OperatorClassRelationId, AccessShareLock);
    1748           39 :         scan = systable_beginscan(rel, OpclassOidIndexId, indexOK,
    1749              :                                                           NULL, 1, skey);
    1750              : 
    1751           39 :         if (HeapTupleIsValid(htup = systable_getnext(scan)))
    1752              :         {
    1753           39 :                 Form_pg_opclass opclassform = (Form_pg_opclass) GETSTRUCT(htup);
    1754              : 
    1755           39 :                 opcentry->opcfamily = opclassform->opcfamily;
    1756           39 :                 opcentry->opcintype = opclassform->opcintype;
    1757              :         }
    1758              :         else
    1759            0 :                 elog(ERROR, "could not find tuple for opclass %u", operatorClassOid);
    1760              : 
    1761           39 :         systable_endscan(scan);
    1762           39 :         table_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
    1763              : 
    1764              :         /*
    1765              :          * Scan pg_amproc to obtain support procs for the opclass.  We only fetch
    1766              :          * the default ones (those with lefttype = righttype = opcintype).
    1767              :          */
    1768           39 :         if (numSupport > 0)
    1769              :         {
    1770           39 :                 ScanKeyInit(&skey[0],
    1771              :                                         Anum_pg_amproc_amprocfamily,
    1772              :                                         BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
    1773           39 :                                         ObjectIdGetDatum(opcentry->opcfamily));
    1774           39 :                 ScanKeyInit(&skey[1],
    1775              :                                         Anum_pg_amproc_amproclefttype,
    1776              :                                         BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
    1777           39 :                                         ObjectIdGetDatum(opcentry->opcintype));
    1778           39 :                 ScanKeyInit(&skey[2],
    1779              :                                         Anum_pg_amproc_amprocrighttype,
    1780              :                                         BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
    1781           39 :                                         ObjectIdGetDatum(opcentry->opcintype));
    1782           39 :                 rel = table_open(AccessMethodProcedureRelationId, AccessShareLock);
    1783           39 :                 scan = systable_beginscan(rel, AccessMethodProcedureIndexId, indexOK,
    1784              :                                                                   NULL, 3, skey);
    1785              : 
    1786          151 :                 while (HeapTupleIsValid(htup = systable_getnext(scan)))
    1787              :                 {
    1788          112 :                         Form_pg_amproc amprocform = (Form_pg_amproc) GETSTRUCT(htup);
    1789              : 
    1790          112 :                         if (amprocform->amprocnum <= 0 ||
    1791          112 :                                 (StrategyNumber) amprocform->amprocnum > numSupport)
    1792            0 :                                 elog(ERROR, "invalid amproc number %d for opclass %u",
    1793              :                                          amprocform->amprocnum, operatorClassOid);
    1794              : 
    1795          112 :                         opcentry->supportProcs[amprocform->amprocnum - 1] =
    1796          112 :                                 amprocform->amproc;
    1797              :                 }
    1798              : 
    1799           39 :                 systable_endscan(scan);
    1800           39 :                 table_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
    1801              :         }
    1802              : 
    1803           39 :         opcentry->valid = true;
    1804           39 :         return opcentry;
    1805              : }

    1807              : /*

    1822         2188 : RelationInitTableAccessMethod(Relation relation)

    1866              : 
    1867              : /*
    1868              :  *              formrdesc
    1869              :  *
    1870              :  *              This is a special cut-down version of RelationBuildDesc(),
    1871              :  *              used while initializing the relcache.
    1872              :  *              The relation descriptor is built just from the supplied parameters,
    1873              :  *              without actually looking at any system table entries.  We cheat
    1874              :  *              quite a lot since we only need to work for a few basic system
    1875              :  *              catalogs.
    1876              :  *
    1877              :  * The catalogs this is used for can't have constraints (except attnotnull),
    1878              :  * default values, rules, or triggers, since we don't cope with any of that.
    1879              :  * (Well, actually, this only matters for properties that need to be valid
    1880              :  * during bootstrap or before RelationCacheInitializePhase3 runs, and none of
    1881              :  * these properties matter then...)
    1882              :  *
    1883              :  * NOTE: we assume we are already switched into CacheMemoryContext.
    1884              :  */
    1885              : static void
    1886           36 : formrdesc(const char *relationName, Oid relationReltype,
    1887              :                   bool isshared,
    1888              :                   int natts, const FormData_pg_attribute *attrs)
    1889              : {
    1890              :         Relation        relation;
    1891              :         int                     i;
    1892              :         bool            has_not_null;
    1893              : 
    1894              :         /*
    1895              :          * allocate new relation desc, clear all fields of reldesc
    1896              :          */
    1897           36 :         relation = (Relation) palloc0(sizeof(RelationData));
    1898              : 
    1899              :         /* make sure relation is marked as having no open file yet */
    1900           36 :         relation->rd_smgr = NULL;
    1901              : 
    1902              :         /*
    1903              :          * initialize reference count: 1 because it is nailed in cache
    1904              :          */
    1905           36 :         relation->rd_refcnt = 1;
    1906              : 
    1907              :         /*
    1908              :          * all entries built with this routine are nailed-in-cache; none are for
    1909              :          * new or temp relations.
    1910              :          */
    1911           36 :         relation->rd_isnailed = true;
    1912           36 :         relation->rd_createSubid = InvalidSubTransactionId;
    1913           36 :         relation->rd_newRelfilenodeSubid = InvalidSubTransactionId;
    1914           36 :         relation->rd_firstRelfilenodeSubid = InvalidSubTransactionId;
    1915           36 :         relation->rd_droppedSubid = InvalidSubTransactionId;
    1916           36 :         relation->rd_backend = InvalidBackendId;
    1917           36 :         relation->rd_islocaltemp = false;
    1918              : 
    1919              :         /*
    1920              :          * initialize relation tuple form
    1921              :          *
    1922              :          * The data we insert here is pretty incomplete/bogus, but it'll serve to
    1923              :          * get us launched.  RelationCacheInitializePhase3() will read the real
    1924              :          * data from pg_class and replace what we've done here.  Note in
    1925              :          * particular that relowner is left as zero; this cues
    1926              :          * RelationCacheInitializePhase3 that the real data isn't there yet.
    1927              :          */
    1928           36 :         relation->rd_rel = (Form_pg_class) palloc0(CLASS_TUPLE_SIZE);
    1929              : 
    1930           36 :         namestrcpy(&relation->rd_rel->relname, relationName);
    1931           36 :         relation->rd_rel->relnamespace = PG_CATALOG_NAMESPACE;
    1932           36 :         relation->rd_rel->reltype = relationReltype;
    1933              : 
    1934              :         /*
    1935              :          * It's important to distinguish between shared and non-shared relations,
    1936              :          * even at bootstrap time, to make sure we know where they are stored.
    1937              :          */
    1938           36 :         relation->rd_rel->relisshared = isshared;
    1939           36 :         if (isshared)
    1940           20 :                 relation->rd_rel->reltablespace = GLOBALTABLESPACE_OID;
    1941              : 
    1942              :         /* formrdesc is used only for permanent relations */
    1943           36 :         relation->rd_rel->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_PERMANENT;
    1944              : 
    1945              :         /* ... and they're always populated, too */
    1946           36 :         relation->rd_rel->relispopulated = true;
    1947              : 
    1948           36 :         relation->rd_rel->relreplident = REPLICA_IDENTITY_NOTHING;
    1949           36 :         relation->rd_rel->relpages = 0;
    1950           36 :         relation->rd_rel->reltuples = 0;
    1951           36 :         relation->rd_rel->relallvisible = 0;
    1952           36 :         relation->rd_rel->relkind = RELKIND_RELATION;
    1953           36 :         relation->rd_rel->relnatts = (int16) natts;
    1954           36 :         relation->rd_rel->relam = HEAP_TABLE_AM_OID;
    1955              : 
    1956              :         /*
    1957              :          * initialize attribute tuple form
    1958              :          *
    1959              :          * Unlike the case with the relation tuple, this data had better be right
    1960              :          * because it will never be replaced.  The data comes from
    1961              :          * src/include/catalog/ headers via genbki.pl.
    1962              :          */
    1963           36 :         relation->rd_att = CreateTemplateTupleDesc(natts);
    1964           36 :         relation->rd_att->tdrefcount = 1; /* mark as refcounted */
    1965              : 
    1966           36 :         relation->rd_att->tdtypeid = relationReltype;
    1967           36 :         relation->rd_att->tdtypmod = -1;  /* unnecessary, but... */
    1968              : 
    1969              :         /*
    1970              :          * initialize tuple desc info
    1971              :          */
    1972           36 :         has_not_null = false;
    1973          680 :         for (i = 0; i < natts; i++)
    1974              :         {
    1975          644 :                 memcpy(TupleDescAttr(relation->rd_att, i),
    1976          644 :                            &attrs[i],
    1977              :                            ATTRIBUTE_FIXED_PART_SIZE);
    1978          644 :                 has_not_null |= attrs[i].attnotnull;
    1979              :                 /* make sure attcacheoff is valid */
    1980          644 :                 TupleDescAttr(relation->rd_att, i)->attcacheoff = -1;
    1981              :         }
    1982              : 
    1983              :         /* initialize first attribute's attcacheoff, cf RelationBuildTupleDesc */
    1984           36 :         TupleDescAttr(relation->rd_att, 0)->attcacheoff = 0;
    1985              : 
    1986              :         /* mark not-null status */
    1987           36 :         if (has_not_null)
    1988              :         {
    1989           36 :                 TupleConstr *constr = (TupleConstr *) palloc0(sizeof(TupleConstr));
    1990              : 
    1991           36 :                 constr->has_not_null = true;
    1992           36 :                 relation->rd_att->constr = constr;
    1993              :         }
    1994              : 
    1995              :         /*
    1996              :          * initialize relation id from info in att array (my, this is ugly)
    1997              :          */
    1998           36 :         RelationGetRelid(relation) = TupleDescAttr(relation->rd_att, 0)->attrelid;
    1999              : 
    2000              :         /*
    2001              :          * All relations made with formrdesc are mapped.  This is necessarily so
    2002              :          * because there is no other way to know what filenode they currently
    2003              :          * have.  In bootstrap mode, add them to the initial relation mapper data,
    2004              :          * specifying that the initial filenode is the same as the OID.
    2005              :          */
    2006           36 :         relation->rd_rel->relfilenode = InvalidOid;
    2007           36 :         if (IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
    2008            4 :                 RelationMapUpdateMap(RelationGetRelid(relation),
    2009              :                                                          RelationGetRelid(relation),
    2010              :                                                          isshared, true);
    2011              : 
    2012              :         /*
    2013              :          * initialize the relation lock manager information
    2014              :          */
    2015           36 :         RelationInitLockInfo(relation); /* see lmgr.c */
    2016              : 
    2017              :         /*
    2018              :          * initialize physical addressing information for the relation
    2019              :          */
    2020           36 :         RelationInitPhysicalAddr(relation);
    2021              : 
    2022              :         /*
    2023              :          * initialize the table am handler

    2026           36 :         relation->rd_tableam = GetHeapamTableAmRoutine();
    2027              : 
    2028              :         /*

    2051              : 
    2052              : /* ----------------------------------------------------------------
    2053              :  *                               Relation Descriptor Lookup Interface
    2054              :  * ----------------------------------------------------------------
    2055              :  */
    2056              : 
    2057              : /*
    2058              :  *              RelationIdGetRelation
    2059              :  *
    2060              :  *              Lookup a reldesc by OID; make one if not already in cache.
    2061              :  *
    2062              :  *              Returns NULL if no pg_class row could be found for the given relid
    2063              :  *              (suggesting we are trying to access a just-deleted relation).
    2064              :  *              Any other error is reported via elog.
    2065              :  *
    2066              :  *              NB: caller should already have at least AccessShareLock on the
    2067              :  *              relation ID, else there are nasty race conditions.
    2068              :  *
    2069              :  *              NB: relation ref count is incremented, or set to 1 if new entry.
    2070              :  *              Caller should eventually decrement count.  (Usually,
    2071              :  *              that happens by calling RelationClose().)
    2072              :  */
    2073              : Relation
    2074       109745 : RelationIdGetRelation(Oid relationId)
    2075              : {
    2076              :         Relation        rd;
    2077              : 
    2078              :         /* Make sure we're in an xact, even if this ends up being a cache hit */
    2079              :         Assert(IsTransactionState());
    2080              : 
    2081              :         /*
    2082              :          * first try to find reldesc in the cache
    2083              :          */
    2084       109745 :         RelationIdCacheLookup(relationId, rd);
    2085              : 
    2086       109745 :         if (RelationIsValid(rd))
    2087              :         {
    2088              :                 /* return NULL for dropped relations */
    2089       108179 :                 if (rd->rd_droppedSubid != InvalidSubTransactionId)
    2090              :                 {
    2091              :                         Assert(!rd->rd_isvalid);
    2092            0 :                         return NULL;
    2093              :                 }
    2094              : 
    2095       108179 :                 RelationIncrementReferenceCount(rd);
    2096              :                 /* revalidate cache entry if necessary */
    2097       108179 :                 if (!rd->rd_isvalid)
    2098              :                 {
    2099              :                         /*
    2100              :                          * Indexes only have a limited number of possible schema changes,
    2101              :                          * and we don't want to use the full-blown procedure because it's
    2102              :                          * a headache for indexes that reload itself depends on.
    2103              :                          */
    2104         1089 :                         if (rd->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_INDEX ||
    2105         1084 :                                 rd->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_INDEX)
    2106            5 :                                 RelationReloadIndexInfo(rd);
    2107              :                         else
    2108         1084 :                                 RelationClearRelation(rd, true);
    2109              : 
    2110              :                         /*
    2111              :                          * Normally entries need to be valid here, but before the relcache
    2112              :                          * has been initialized, not enough infrastructure exists to
    2113              :                          * perform pg_class lookups. The structure of such entries doesn't
    2114              :                          * change, but we still want to update the rd_rel entry. So
    2115              :                          * rd_isvalid = false is left in place for a later lookup.
    2116              :                          */
    2117              :                         Assert(rd->rd_isvalid ||
    2118              :                                    (rd->rd_isnailed && !criticalRelcachesBuilt));
    2119              :                 }
    2120       108179 :                 return rd;
    2121              :         }
    2122              : 
    2123              :         /*
    2124              :          * no reldesc in the cache, so have RelationBuildDesc() build one and add
    2125              :          * it.
    2126              :          */
    2127         1566 :         rd = RelationBuildDesc(relationId, true);
    2128         1566 :         if (RelationIsValid(rd))
    2129         1566 :                 RelationIncrementReferenceCount(rd);
    2130         1566 :         return rd;
    2131              : }
    2132              : 
    2133              : /* ----------------------------------------------------------------
    2134              :  *                              cache invalidation support routines
    2135              :  * ----------------------------------------------------------------
    2136              :  */
    2137              : 
    2138              : /*
    2139              :  * RelationIncrementReferenceCount
    2140              :  *              Increments relation reference count.
    2141              :  *
    2142              :  * Note: bootstrap mode has its own weird ideas about relation refcount
    2143              :  * behavior; we ought to fix it someday, but for now, just disable
    2144              :  * reference count ownership tracking in bootstrap mode.
    2145              :  */
    2146              : void
    2147       158696 : RelationIncrementReferenceCount(Relation rel)
    2148              : {
    2149       158696 :         ResourceOwnerEnlargeRelationRefs(CurrentResourceOwner);
    2150       158696 :         rel->rd_refcnt += 1;
    2151       158696 :         if (!IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
    2152       153608 :                 ResourceOwnerRememberRelationRef(CurrentResourceOwner, rel);
    2153       158696 : }
    2154              : 
    2155              : /*
    2156              :  * RelationDecrementReferenceCount
    2157              :  *              Decrements relation reference count.
    2158              :  */
    2159              : void
    2160       158696 : RelationDecrementReferenceCount(Relation rel)
    2161              : {
    2162              :         Assert(rel->rd_refcnt > 0);
    2163       158696 :         rel->rd_refcnt -= 1;
    2164       158696 :         if (!IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
    2165       153608 :                 ResourceOwnerForgetRelationRef(CurrentResourceOwner, rel);
    2166       158696 : }
    2167              : 
    2168              : /*
    2169              :  * RelationClose - close an open relation
    2170              :  *
    2171              :  *      Actually, we just decrement the refcount.
    2172              :  *
    2173              :  *      NOTE: if compiled with -DRELCACHE_FORCE_RELEASE then relcache entries
    2174              :  *      will be freed as soon as their refcount goes to zero.  In combination
    2175              :  *      with aset.c's CLOBBER_FREED_MEMORY option, this provides a good test
    2176              :  *      to catch references to already-released relcache entries.  It slows
    2177              :  *      things down quite a bit, however.
    2178              :  */
    2179              : void
    2180       110530 : RelationClose(Relation relation)
    2181              : {
    2182              :         /* Note: no locking manipulations needed */
    2183       110530 :         RelationDecrementReferenceCount(relation);
    2184              : 
    2185              :         /*
    2186              :          * If the relation is no longer open in this session, we can clean up any
    2187              :          * stale partition descriptors it has.  This is unlikely, so check to see
    2188              :          * if there are child contexts before expending a call to mcxt.c.
    2189              :          */
    2190       110530 :         if (RelationHasReferenceCountZero(relation) &&
    2191        71548 :                 relation->rd_pdcxt != NULL &&
    2192          174 :                 relation->rd_pdcxt->firstchild != NULL)
    2193           27 :                 MemoryContextDeleteChildren(relation->rd_pdcxt);
    2194              : 
    2195              : #ifdef RELCACHE_FORCE_RELEASE
    2196              :         if (RelationHasReferenceCountZero(relation) &&
    2197              :                 relation->rd_createSubid == InvalidSubTransactionId &&
    2198              :                 relation->rd_firstRelfilenodeSubid == InvalidSubTransactionId)
    2199              :                 RelationClearRelation(relation, false);
    2200              : #endif
    2201       110530 : }
    2202              : 
    2203              : /*
    2204              :  * RelationReloadIndexInfo - reload minimal information for an open index
    2205              :  *
    2206              :  *      This function is used only for indexes.  A relcache inval on an index
    2207              :  *      can mean that its pg_class or pg_index row changed.  There are only
    2208              :  *      very limited changes that are allowed to an existing index's schema,
    2209              :  *      so we can update the relcache entry without a complete rebuild; which
    2210              :  *      is fortunate because we can't rebuild an index entry that is "nailed"
    2211              :  *      and/or in active use.  We support full replacement of the pg_class row,
    2212              :  *      as well as updates of a few simple fields of the pg_index row.
    2213              :  *
    2214              :  *      We can't necessarily reread the catalog rows right away; we might be
    2215              :  *      in a failed transaction when we receive the SI notification.  If so,
    2216              :  *      RelationClearRelation just marks the entry as invalid by setting
    2217              :  *      rd_isvalid to false.  This routine is called to fix the entry when it
    2218              :  *      is next needed.
    2219              :  *
    2220              :  *      We assume that at the time we are called, we have at least AccessShareLock
    2221              :  *      on the target index.  (Note: in the calls from RelationClearRelation,
    2222              :  *      this is legitimate because we know the rel has positive refcount.)
    2223              :  *
    2224              :  *      If the target index is an index on pg_class or pg_index, we'd better have
    2225              :  *      previously gotten at least AccessShareLock on its underlying catalog,
    2226              :  *      else we are at risk of deadlock against someone trying to exclusive-lock
    2227              :  *      the heap and index in that order.  This is ensured in current usage by
    2228              :  *      only applying this to indexes being opened or having positive refcount.
    2229              :  */
    2230              : static void
    2231          159 : RelationReloadIndexInfo(Relation relation)
    2232              : {
    2233              :         bool            indexOK;
    2234              :         HeapTuple       pg_class_tuple;
    2235              :         Form_pg_class relp;
    2236              : 
    2237              :         /* Should be called only for invalidated, live indexes */
    2238              :         Assert((relation->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_INDEX ||
    2239              :                         relation->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_INDEX) &&
    2240              :                    !relation->rd_isvalid &&
    2241              :                    relation->rd_droppedSubid == InvalidSubTransactionId);
    2242              : 
    2243              :         /* Ensure it's closed at smgr level */
    2244          159 :         RelationCloseSmgr(relation);
    2245              : 
    2246              :         /* Must free any AM cached data upon relcache flush */
    2247          159 :         if (relation->rd_amcache)
    2248            0 :                 pfree(relation->rd_amcache);
    2249          159 :         relation->rd_amcache = NULL;
    2250              : 
    2251              :         /*
    2252              :          * If it's a shared index, we might be called before backend startup has
    2253              :          * finished selecting a database, in which case we have no way to read
    2254              :          * pg_class yet.  However, a shared index can never have any significant
    2255              :          * schema updates, so it's okay to ignore the invalidation signal.  Just
    2256              :          * mark it valid and return without doing anything more.
    2257              :          */
    2258          159 :         if (relation->rd_rel->relisshared && !criticalRelcachesBuilt)
    2259              :         {
    2260            0 :                 relation->rd_isvalid = true;
    2261            0 :                 return;
    2262              :         }
    2263              : 
    2264              :         /*
    2265              :          * Read the pg_class row
    2266              :          *
    2267              :          * Don't try to use an indexscan of pg_class_oid_index to reload the info
    2268              :          * for pg_class_oid_index ...
    2269              :          */
    2270          159 :         indexOK = (RelationGetRelid(relation) != ClassOidIndexId);
    2271          159 :         pg_class_tuple = ScanPgRelation(RelationGetRelid(relation), indexOK, false);
    2272          159 :         if (!HeapTupleIsValid(pg_class_tuple))
    2273            0 :                 elog(ERROR, "could not find pg_class tuple for index %u",
    2274              :                          RelationGetRelid(relation));
    2275          159 :         relp = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(pg_class_tuple);
    2276          159 :         memcpy(relation->rd_rel, relp, CLASS_TUPLE_SIZE);
    2277              :         /* Reload reloptions in case they changed */
    2278          159 :         if (relation->rd_options)
    2279            0 :                 pfree(relation->rd_options);
    2280          159 :         RelationParseRelOptions(relation, pg_class_tuple);
    2281              :         /* done with pg_class tuple */
    2282          159 :         heap_freetuple(pg_class_tuple);
    2283              :         /* We must recalculate physical address in case it changed */
    2284          159 :         RelationInitPhysicalAddr(relation);
    2285              : 
    2286              :         /*
    2287              :          * For a non-system index, there are fields of the pg_index row that are
    2288              :          * allowed to change, so re-read that row and update the relcache entry.
    2289              :          * Most of the info derived from pg_index (such as support function lookup
    2290              :          * info) cannot change, and indeed the whole point of this routine is to
    2291              :          * update the relcache entry without clobbering that data; so wholesale
    2292              :          * replacement is not appropriate.
    2293              :          */
    2294          159 :         if (!IsSystemRelation(relation))
    2295              :         {
    2296              :                 HeapTuple       tuple;
    2297              :                 Form_pg_index index;
    2298              : 
    2299          106 :                 tuple = SearchSysCache1(INDEXRELID,
    2300          106 :                                                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(relation)));
    2301          106 :                 if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
    2302            0 :                         elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for index %u",
    2303              :                                  RelationGetRelid(relation));
    2304          106 :                 index = (Form_pg_index) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
    2305              : 
    2306              :                 /*
    2307              :                  * Basically, let's just copy all the bool fields.  There are one or
    2308              :                  * two of these that can't actually change in the current code, but

    2310              :                  * the array fields are allowed to change, though.
    2311              :                  */
    2312          106 :                 relation->rd_index->indisunique = index->indisunique;
    2313          106 :                 relation->rd_index->indisprimary = index->indisprimary;
    2314          106 :                 relation->rd_index->indisexclusion = index->indisexclusion;
    2315          106 :                 relation->rd_index->indimmediate = index->indimmediate;
    2316          106 :                 relation->rd_index->indisclustered = index->indisclustered;
    2317          106 :                 relation->rd_index->indisvalid = index->indisvalid;
    2318          106 :                 relation->rd_index->indcheckxmin = index->indcheckxmin;
    2319          106 :                 relation->rd_index->indisready = index->indisready;
    2320          106 :                 relation->rd_index->indislive = index->indislive;
    2321          106 :                 relation->rd_index->indisreplident = index->indisreplident;
    2322              : 
    2323              :                 /* Copy xmin too, as that is needed to make sense of indcheckxmin */
    2324          106 :                 HeapTupleHeaderSetXmin(relation->rd_indextuple->t_data,
    2325              :                                                            HeapTupleHeaderGetXmin(tuple->t_data));
    2326              : 
    2327          106 :                 ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
    2328              :         }
    2329              : 
    2330              :         /* Okay, now it's valid again */
    2331          159 :         relation->rd_isvalid = true;
    2332              : }
    2333              : 
    2334              : /*
    2335              :  * RelationReloadNailed - reload minimal information for nailed relations.
    2336              :  *
    2337              :  * The structure of a nailed relation can never change (which is good, because
    2338              :  * we rely on knowing their structure to be able to read catalog content). But
    2339              :  * some parts, e.g. pg_class.relfrozenxid, are still important to have
    2340              :  * accurate content for. Therefore those need to be reloaded after the arrival
    2341              :  * of invalidations.
    2342              :  */
    2343              : static void
    2344         1159 : RelationReloadNailed(Relation relation)
    2345              : {
    2346              :         Assert(relation->rd_isnailed);
    2347              : 
    2348              :         /*
    2349              :          * Redo RelationInitPhysicalAddr in case it is a mapped relation whose
    2350              :          * mapping changed.
    2351              :          */
    2352         1159 :         RelationInitPhysicalAddr(relation);
    2353              : 
    2354              :         /* flag as needing to be revalidated */
    2355         1159 :         relation->rd_isvalid = false;
    2356              : 
    2357              :         /*
    2358              :          * Can only reread catalog contents if in a transaction.  If the relation
    2359              :          * is currently open (not counting the nailed refcount), do so
    2360              :          * immediately. Otherwise we've already marked the entry as possibly
    2361              :          * invalid, and it'll be fixed when next opened.
    2362              :          */
    2363         1159 :         if (!IsTransactionState() || relation->rd_refcnt <= 1)
    2364           52 :                 return;
    2365              : 
    2366         1107 :         if (relation->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_INDEX)
    2367              :         {
    2368              :                 /*
    2369              :                  * If it's a nailed-but-not-mapped index, then we need to re-read the
    2370              :                  * pg_class row to see if its relfilenode changed.
    2371              :                  */
    2372            0 :                 RelationReloadIndexInfo(relation);
    2373              :         }
    2374              :         else
    2375              :         {
    2376              :                 /*
    2377              :                  * Reload a non-index entry.  We can't easily do so if relcaches
    2378              :                  * aren't yet built, but that's fine because at that stage the
    2379              :                  * attributes that need to be current (like relfrozenxid) aren't yet
    2380              :                  * accessed.  To ensure the entry will later be revalidated, we leave
    2381              :                  * it in invalid state, but allow use (cf. RelationIdGetRelation()).
    2382              :                  */
    2383         1107 :                 if (criticalRelcachesBuilt)
    2384              :                 {
    2385              :                         HeapTuple       pg_class_tuple;
    2386              :                         Form_pg_class relp;
    2387              : 
    2388              :                         /*
    2389              :                          * NB: Mark the entry as valid before starting to scan, to avoid
    2390              :                          * self-recursion when re-building pg_class.
    2391              :                          */
    2392           23 :                         relation->rd_isvalid = true;
    2393              : 
    2394           23 :                         pg_class_tuple = ScanPgRelation(RelationGetRelid(relation),
    2395              :                                                                                         true, false);
    2396           23 :                         relp = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(pg_class_tuple);
    2397           23 :                         memcpy(relation->rd_rel, relp, CLASS_TUPLE_SIZE);
    2398           23 :                         heap_freetuple(pg_class_tuple);
    2399              : 
    2400              :                         /*
    2401              :                          * Again mark as valid, to protect against concurrently arriving
    2402              :                          * invalidations.
    2403              :                          */
    2404           23 :                         relation->rd_isvalid = true;
    2405              :                 }
    2406              :         }
    2407              : }
    2408              : 
    2409              : /*
    2410              :  * RelationDestroyRelation
    2411              :  *
    2412              :  *      Physically delete a relation cache entry and all subsidiary data.
    2413              :  *      Caller must already have unhooked the entry from the hash table.
    2414              :  */
    2415              : static void
    2416         3196 : RelationDestroyRelation(Relation relation, bool remember_tupdesc)
    2417              : {
    2418              :         Assert(RelationHasReferenceCountZero(relation));
    2419              : 
    2420              :         /*
    2421              :          * Make sure smgr and lower levels close the relation's files, if they
    2422              :          * weren't closed already.  (This was probably done by caller, but let's
    2423              :          * just be real sure.)
    2424              :          */
    2425         3196 :         RelationCloseSmgr(relation);
    2426              : 
    2427              :         /*
    2428              :          * Free all the subsidiary data structures of the relcache entry, then the
    2429              :          * entry itself.
    2430              :          */
    2431         3196 :         if (relation->rd_rel)
    2432         3196 :                 pfree(relation->rd_rel);
    2433              :         /* can't use DecrTupleDescRefCount here */
    2434              :         Assert(relation->rd_att->tdrefcount > 0);
    2435         3196 :         if (--relation->rd_att->tdrefcount == 0)
    2436              :         {
    2437              :                 /*
    2438              :                  * If we Rebuilt a relcache entry during a transaction then its
    2439              :                  * possible we did that because the TupDesc changed as the result of
    2440              :                  * an ALTER TABLE that ran at less than AccessExclusiveLock. It's
    2441              :                  * possible someone copied that TupDesc, in which case the copy would
    2442              :                  * point to free'd memory. So if we rebuild an entry we keep the
    2443              :                  * TupDesc around until end of transaction, to be safe.
    2444              :                  */
    2445         3191 :                 if (remember_tupdesc)
    2446          169 :                         RememberToFreeTupleDescAtEOX(relation->rd_att);
    2447              :                 else
    2448         3022 :                         FreeTupleDesc(relation->rd_att);
    2449              :         }
    2450         3196 :         FreeTriggerDesc(relation->trigdesc);
    2451         3196 :         list_free_deep(relation->rd_fkeylist);
    2452         3196 :         list_free(relation->rd_indexlist);
    2453         3196 :         list_free(relation->rd_statlist);
    2454         3196 :         bms_free(relation->rd_indexattr);
    2455         3196 :         bms_free(relation->rd_keyattr);
    2456         3196 :         bms_free(relation->rd_pkattr);
    2457         3196 :         bms_free(relation->rd_idattr);
    2458         3196 :         if (relation->rd_pubactions)
    2459           13 :                 pfree(relation->rd_pubactions);
    2460         3196 :         if (relation->rd_options)
    2461           25 :                 pfree(relation->rd_options);
    2462         3196 :         if (relation->rd_indextuple)
    2463          317 :                 pfree(relation->rd_indextuple);
    2464         3196 :         if (relation->rd_amcache)
    2465            0 :                 pfree(relation->rd_amcache);
    2466         3196 :         if (relation->rd_fdwroutine)
    2467            0 :                 pfree(relation->rd_fdwroutine);
    2468         3196 :         if (relation->rd_indexcxt)
    2469          317 :                 MemoryContextDelete(relation->rd_indexcxt);
    2470         3196 :         if (relation->rd_rulescxt)
    2471          237 :                 MemoryContextDelete(relation->rd_rulescxt);
    2472         3196 :         if (relation->rd_rsdesc)
    2473            0 :                 MemoryContextDelete(relation->rd_rsdesc->rscxt);
    2474         3196 :         if (relation->rd_partkeycxt)

    2476         3196 :         if (relation->rd_pdcxt)

    2482              : 
    2483              : /*
    2484              :  * RelationClearRelation
    2485              :  *
    2486              :  *       Physically blow away a relation cache entry, or reset it and rebuild
    2487              :  *       it from scratch (that is, from catalog entries).  The latter path is
    2488              :  *       used when we are notified of a change to an open relation (one with
    2489              :  *       refcount > 0).
    2490              :  *
    2491              :  *       NB: when rebuilding, we'd better hold some lock on the relation,
    2492              :  *       else the catalog data we need to read could be changing under us.
    2493              :  *       Also, a rel to be rebuilt had better have refcnt > 0.  This is because
    2494              :  *       a sinval reset could happen while we're accessing the catalogs, and
    2495              :  *       the rel would get blown away underneath us by RelationCacheInvalidate
    2496              :  *       if it has zero refcnt.
    2497              :  *
    2498              :  *       The "rebuild" parameter is redundant in current usage because it has
    2499              :  *       to match the relation's refcnt status, but we keep it as a crosscheck
    2500              :  *       that we're doing what the caller expects.
    2501              :  */
    2502              : static void
    2503         4521 : RelationClearRelation(Relation relation, bool rebuild)
    2504              : {
    2505              :         /*
    2506              :          * As per notes above, a rel to be rebuilt MUST have refcnt > 0; while of
    2507              :          * course it would be an equally bad idea to blow away one with nonzero
    2508              :          * refcnt, since that would leave someone somewhere with a dangling

    2538              : 
    2539              :         /* Mark it invalid until we've finished rebuild */

    2602              :                 /*
    2603              :                  * Our strategy for rebuilding an open relcache entry is to build a

    2610              :                  * before, so any code that might expect to continue accessing it

    2612              :                  * subtransaction that ALTERs a table and then gets canceled partway
    2613              :                  * through the cache entry rebuild.  The outer transaction should

    2784              : /*
    2785              :  * RelationFlushRelation

    2791         3026 : RelationFlushRelation(Relation relation)

    2794         1655 :                 relation->rd_firstRelfilenodeSubid != InvalidSubTransactionId)

    2852              : /*

    2889              : /*
    2890              :  * RelationCacheInvalidate
    2891              :  *       Blow away cached relation descriptors that have zero reference counts,
    2892              :  *       and rebuild those with positive reference counts.  Also reset the smgr
    2893              :  *       relation cache and re-read relation mapping data.
    2894              :  *
    2895              :  *       Apart from debug_discard_caches, this is currently used only to recover
    2896              :  *       from SI message buffer overflow, so we do not touch relations having
    2897              :  *       new-in-transaction relfilenodes; they cannot be targets of cross-backend
    2898              :  *       SI updates (and our own updates now go through a separate linked list
    2899              :  *       that isn't limited by the SI message buffer size).
    2900              :  *
    2901              :  *       We do this in two phases: the first pass deletes deletable items, and
    2902              :  *       the second one rebuilds the rebuildable items.  This is essential for
    2903              :  *       safety, because hash_seq_search only copes with concurrent deletion of
    2904              :  *       the element it is currently visiting.  If a second SI overflow were to
    2905              :  *       occur while we are walking the table, resulting in recursive entry to
    2906              :  *       this routine, we could crash because the inner invocation blows away
    2907              :  *       the entry next to be visited by the outer scan.  But this way is OK,
    2908              :  *       because (a) during the first pass we won't process any more SI messages,
    2909              :  *       so hash_seq_search will complete safely; (b) during the second pass we
    2910              :  *       only hold onto pointers to nondeletable entries.
    2911              :  *
    2912              :  *       The two-phase approach also makes it easy to update relfilenodes for
    2913              :  *       mapped relations before we do anything else, and to ensure that the
    2914              :  *       second pass processes nailed-in-cache items before other nondeletable
    2915              :  *       items.  This should ensure that system catalogs are up to date before
    2916              :  *       we attempt to use them to reload information about other open relations.
    2917              :  *
    2918              :  *       After those two phases of work having immediate effects, we normally
    2919              :  *       signal any RelationBuildDesc() on the stack to start over.  However, we
    2920              :  *       don't do this if called as part of debug_discard_caches.  Otherwise,
    2921              :  *       RelationBuildDesc() would become an infinite loop.
    2922              :  */
    2923              : void
    2924            2 : RelationCacheInvalidate(bool debug_discard)
    2925              : {
    2926              :         HASH_SEQ_STATUS status;
    2927              :         RelIdCacheEnt *idhentry;
    2928              :         Relation        relation;
    2929            2 :         List       *rebuildFirstList = NIL;
    2930            2 :         List       *rebuildList = NIL;
    2931              :         ListCell   *l;
    2932              :         int                     i;
    2933              : 
    2934              :         /*
    2935              :          * Reload relation mapping data before starting to reconstruct cache.
    2936              :          */
    2937            2 :         RelationMapInvalidateAll();
    2938              : 
    2939              :         /* Phase 1 */
    2940            2 :         hash_seq_init(&status, RelationIdCache);
    2941              : 
    2942           11 :         while ((idhentry = (RelIdCacheEnt *) hash_seq_search(&status)) != NULL)
    2943              :         {
    2944            9 :                 relation = idhentry->reldesc;
    2945              : 
    2946              :                 /* Must close all smgr references to avoid leaving dangling ptrs */
    2947            9 :                 RelationCloseSmgr(relation);
    2948              : 
    2949              :                 /*
    2950              :                  * Ignore new relations; no other backend will manipulate them before
    2951              :                  * we commit.  Likewise, before replacing a relation's relfilenode, we
    2952              :                  * shall have acquired AccessExclusiveLock and drained any applicable
    2953              :                  * pending invalidations.
    2954              :                  */
    2955            9 :                 if (relation->rd_createSubid != InvalidSubTransactionId ||
    2956            9 :                         relation->rd_firstRelfilenodeSubid != InvalidSubTransactionId)
    2957            0 :                         continue;
    2958              : 
    2959            9 :                 relcacheInvalsReceived++;
    2960              : 
    2961            9 :                 if (RelationHasReferenceCountZero(relation))
    2962              :                 {
    2963              :                         /* Delete this entry immediately */
    2964              :                         Assert(!relation->rd_isnailed);
    2965            0 :                         RelationClearRelation(relation, false);
    2966              :                 }
    2967              :                 else
    2968              :                 {
    2969              :                         /*
    2970              :                          * If it's a mapped relation, immediately update its rd_node in
    2971              :                          * case its relfilenode changed.  We must do this during phase 1
    2972              :                          * in case the relation is consulted during rebuild of other
    2973              :                          * relcache entries in phase 2.  It's safe since consulting the
    2974              :                          * map doesn't involve any access to relcache entries.
    2975              :                          */
    2976            9 :                         if (RelationIsMapped(relation))
    2977            9 :                                 RelationInitPhysicalAddr(relation);
    2978              : 
    2979              :                         /*

    3073              : #ifdef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
    3074              : static void
    3075              : AssertPendingSyncConsistency(Relation relation)

    3091              : /*
    3092              :  * AssertPendingSyncs_RelationCache
    3093              :  *
    3094              :  *      Assert that relcache.c and storage.c agree on whether to skip WAL.
