    3096              : void
    3097              : AssertPendingSyncs_RelationCache(void)
    3098              : {
    3099              :         HASH_SEQ_STATUS status;
    3100              :         LOCALLOCK  *locallock;
    3101              :         Relation   *rels;
    3102              :         int                     maxrels;
    3103              :         int                     nrels;
    3104              :         RelIdCacheEnt *idhentry;
    3105              :         int                     i;
    3106              : 
    3107              :         /*
    3108              :          * Open every relation that this transaction has locked.  If, for some
    3109              :          * relation, storage.c is skipping WAL and relcache.c is not skipping WAL,
    3110              :          * a CommandCounterIncrement() typically yields a local invalidation

    3112              :          * here, we detect the problem.
    3113              :          */

    3115              :         maxrels = 1;
    3116              :         rels = palloc(maxrels * sizeof(*rels));

    3118              :         hash_seq_init(&status, GetLockMethodLocalHash());
    3119              :         while ((locallock = (LOCALLOCK *) hash_seq_search(&status)) != NULL)
    3120              :         {
    3121              :                 Oid                     relid;
    3122              :                 Relation        r;
    3123              : 
    3124              :                 if (locallock->nLocks <= 0)
    3125              :                         continue;
    3126              :                 if ((LockTagType) locallock->tag.lock.locktag_type !=
    3127              :                         LOCKTAG_RELATION)
    3128              :                         continue;
    3129              :                 relid = ObjectIdGetDatum(locallock->tag.lock.locktag_field2);
    3130              :                 r = RelationIdGetRelation(relid);
    3131              :                 if (!RelationIsValid(r))
    3132              :                         continue;
    3133              :                 if (nrels >= maxrels)
    3134              :                 {
    3135              :                         maxrels *= 2;
    3136              :                         rels = repalloc(rels, maxrels * sizeof(*rels));
    3137              :                 }
    3138              :                 rels[nrels++] = r;
    3139              :         }
    3140              : 
    3141              :         hash_seq_init(&status, RelationIdCache);

    3144              : 
    3145              :         for (i = 0; i < nrels; i++)

    3147              :         PopActiveSnapshot();
    3148              : }
    3149              : #endif

    3151              : /*
    3152              :  * AtEOXact_RelationCache
    3153              :  *
    3154              :  *      Clean up the relcache at main-transaction commit or abort.
    3155              :  *
    3156              :  * Note: this must be called *before* processing invalidation messages.
    3157              :  * In the case of abort, we don't want to try to rebuild any invalidated
    3158              :  * cache entries (since we can't safely do database accesses).  Therefore
    3159              :  * we must reset refcnts before handling pending invalidations.
    3160              :  *
    3161              :  * As of PostgreSQL 8.1, relcache refcnts should get released by the
    3162              :  * ResourceOwner mechanism.  This routine just does a debugging
    3163              :  * cross-check that no pins remain.  However, we also need to do special
    3164              :  * cleanup when the current transaction created any relations or made use
    3165              :  * of forced index lists.
    3166              :  */
    3167              : void
    3168         1840 : AtEOXact_RelationCache(bool isCommit)
    3169              : {
    3170              :         HASH_SEQ_STATUS status;
    3171              :         RelIdCacheEnt *idhentry;
    3172              :         int                     i;
    3173              : 
    3174              :         /*
    3175              :          * Forget in_progress_list.  This is relevant when we're aborting due to
    3176              :          * an error during RelationBuildDesc().
    3177              :          */
    3178              :         Assert(in_progress_list_len == 0 || !isCommit);
    3179         1840 :         in_progress_list_len = 0;
    3180              : 
    3181              :         /*
    3182              :          * Unless the eoxact_list[] overflowed, we only need to examine the rels
    3183              :          * listed in it.  Otherwise fall back on a hash_seq_search scan.
    3184              :          *
    3185              :          * For simplicity, eoxact_list[] entries are not deleted till end of
    3186              :          * top-level transaction, even though we could remove them at
    3187              :          * subtransaction end in some cases, or remove relations from the list if
    3188              :          * they are cleared for other reasons.  Therefore we should expect the
    3189              :          * case that list entries are not found in the hashtable; if not, there's
    3190              :          * nothing to do for them.
    3191              :          */
    3192         1840 :         if (eoxact_list_overflowed)
    3193              :         {
    3194            1 :                 hash_seq_init(&status, RelationIdCache);
    3195          247 :                 while ((idhentry = (RelIdCacheEnt *) hash_seq_search(&status)) != NULL)
    3196              :                 {
    3197          246 :                         AtEOXact_cleanup(idhentry->reldesc, isCommit);
    3198              :                 }
    3199              :         }
    3200              :         else
    3201              :         {
    3202         2379 :                 for (i = 0; i < eoxact_list_len; i++)
    3203              :                 {
    3204          540 :                         idhentry = (RelIdCacheEnt *) hash_search(RelationIdCache,
    3205          540 :                                                                                                          (void *) &eoxact_list[i],
    3206              :                                                                                                          HASH_FIND,
    3207              :                                                                                                          NULL);
    3208          540 :                         if (idhentry != NULL)
    3209          537 :                                 AtEOXact_cleanup(idhentry->reldesc, isCommit);
    3210              :                 }
    3211              :         }
    3212              : 
    3213         1840 :         if (EOXactTupleDescArrayLen > 0)
    3214              :         {
    3215              :                 Assert(EOXactTupleDescArray != NULL);
    3216          316 :                 for (i = 0; i < NextEOXactTupleDescNum; i++)
    3217          169 :                         FreeTupleDesc(EOXactTupleDescArray[i]);
    3218          147 :                 pfree(EOXactTupleDescArray);
    3219          147 :                 EOXactTupleDescArray = NULL;
    3220              :         }
    3221              : 
    3222              :         /* Now we're out of the transaction and can clear the lists */
    3223         1840 :         eoxact_list_len = 0;
    3224         1840 :         eoxact_list_overflowed = false;
    3225         1840 :         NextEOXactTupleDescNum = 0;

    3229              : /*
    3230              :  * AtEOXact_cleanup
    3231              :  *
    3232              :  *      Clean up a single rel at main-transaction commit or abort
    3233              :  *
    3234              :  * NB: this processing must be idempotent, because EOXactListAdd() doesn't
    3235              :  * bother to prevent duplicate entries in eoxact_list[].
    3236              :  */
    3237              : static void
    3238          783 : AtEOXact_cleanup(Relation relation, bool isCommit)
    3239              : {
    3240          783 :         bool            clear_relcache = false;
    3241              : 
    3242              :         /*
    3243              :          * The relcache entry's ref count should be back to its normal
    3244              :          * not-in-a-transaction state: 0 unless it's nailed in cache.
    3245              :          *
    3246              :          * In bootstrap mode, this is NOT true, so don't check it --- the
    3247              :          * bootstrap code expects relations to stay open across start/commit
    3248              :          * transaction calls.  (That seems bogus, but it's not worth fixing.)
    3249              :          *
    3250              :          * Note: ideally this check would be applied to every relcache entry, not
    3251              :          * just those that have eoxact work to do.  But it's not worth forcing a
    3252              :          * scan of the whole relcache just for this.  (Moreover, doing so would
    3253              :          * mean that assert-enabled testing never tests the hash_search code path
    3254              :          * above, which seems a bad idea.)
    3255              :          */
    3256              : #ifdef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
    3257              :         if (!IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
    3258              :         {
    3259              :                 int                     expected_refcnt;
    3260              : 
    3261              :                 expected_refcnt = relation->rd_isnailed ? 1 : 0;
    3262              :                 Assert(relation->rd_refcnt == expected_refcnt);
    3263              :         }
    3264              : #endif
    3265              : 
    3266              :         /*
    3267              :          * Is the relation live after this transaction ends?
    3268              :          *
    3269              :          * During commit, clear the relcache entry if it is preserved after
    3270              :          * relation drop, in order not to orphan the entry.  During rollback,
    3271              :          * clear the relcache entry if the relation is created in the current
    3272              :          * transaction since it isn't interesting any longer once we are out of
    3273              :          * the transaction.
    3274              :          */

    3276              :                 (isCommit ?
    3277          783 :                  relation->rd_droppedSubid != InvalidSubTransactionId :
    3278            0 :                  relation->rd_createSubid != InvalidSubTransactionId);
    3279              : 
    3280              :         /*
    3281              :          * Since we are now out of the transaction, reset the subids to zero. That

    3285          783 :         relation->rd_newRelfilenodeSubid = InvalidSubTransactionId;
    3286          783 :         relation->rd_firstRelfilenodeSubid = InvalidSubTransactionId;
    3287          783 :         relation->rd_droppedSubid = InvalidSubTransactionId;
    3288              : 
    3289          783 :         if (clear_relcache)
    3290              :         {
    3291            6 :                 if (RelationHasReferenceCountZero(relation))
    3292              :                 {
    3293            6 :                         RelationClearRelation(relation, false);
    3294            6 :                         return;
    3295              :                 }
    3296              :                 else
    3297              :                 {
    3298              :                         /*
    3299              :                          * Hmm, somewhere there's a (leaked?) reference to the relation.
    3300              :                          * We daren't remove the entry for fear of dereferencing a
    3301              :                          * dangling pointer later.  Bleat, and mark it as not belonging to
    3302              :                          * the current transaction.  Hopefully it'll get cleaned up
    3303              :                          * eventually.  This must be just a WARNING to avoid
    3304              :                          * error-during-error-recovery loops.
    3305              :                          */
    3306            0 :                         elog(WARNING, "cannot remove relcache entry for \"%s\" because it has nonzero refcount",
    3307              :                                  RelationGetRelationName(relation));
    3308              :                 }
    3309              :         }
    3310              : }

    3312              : /*
    3313              :  * AtEOSubXact_RelationCache
    3314              :  *

    3366              : /*
    3367              :  * AtEOSubXact_cleanup
    3368              :  *
    3369              :  *      Clean up a single rel at subtransaction commit or abort
    3370              :  *
    3371              :  * NB: this processing must be idempotent, because EOXactListAdd() doesn't
    3372              :  * bother to prevent duplicate entries in eoxact_list[].
    3373              :  */
    3374              : static void
    3375            0 : AtEOSubXact_cleanup(Relation relation, bool isCommit,

    3451              : /*
    3452              :  *              RelationBuildLocalRelation

    3457          782 : RelationBuildLocalRelation(const char *relname,
    3458              :                                                    Oid relnamespace,
    3459              :                                                    TupleDesc tupDesc,
    3460              :                                                    Oid relid,

    3688              : /*
    3689              :  * RelationSetNewRelfilenode
    3690              :  *
    3691              :  * Assign a new relfilenode (physical file name), and possibly a new
    3692              :  * persistence setting, to the relation.
    3693              :  *
    3694              :  * This allows a full rewrite of the relation to be done with transactional
    3695              :  * safety (since the filenode assignment can be rolled back).  Note however
    3696              :  * that there is no simple way to access the relation's old data for the
    3697              :  * remainder of the current transaction.  This limits the usefulness to cases
    3698              :  * such as TRUNCATE or rebuilding an index from scratch.
    3699              :  *
    3700              :  * Caller must already hold exclusive lock on the relation.
    3701              :  */

    3703            1 : RelationSetNewRelfilenode(Relation relation, char persistence)
    3704              : {

    3706              :         Relation        pg_class;
    3707              :         ItemPointerData otid;
    3708              :         HeapTuple       tuple;
    3709              :         Form_pg_class classform;
    3710            1 :         MultiXactId minmulti = InvalidMultiXactId;
    3711            1 :         TransactionId freezeXid = InvalidTransactionId;
    3712              :         RelFileNode newrnode;
    3713              : 
    3714              :         /* Allocate a new relfilenode */
    3715            1 :         newrelfilenode = GetNewRelFileNode(relation->rd_rel->reltablespace, NULL,
    3716              :                                                                            persistence);
    3717              : 
    3718              :         /*
    3719              :          * Get a writable copy of the pg_class tuple for the given relation.
    3720              :          */
    3721            1 :         pg_class = table_open(RelationRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
    3722              : 
    3723            1 :         tuple = SearchSysCacheLockedCopy1(RELOID,
    3724            1 :                                                                           ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(relation)));
    3725            1 :         if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
    3726            0 :                 elog(ERROR, "could not find tuple for relation %u",
    3727              :                          RelationGetRelid(relation));
    3728            1 :         otid = tuple->t_self;
    3729            1 :         classform = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
    3730              : 
    3731              :         /*
    3732              :          * Schedule unlinking of the old storage at transaction commit.
    3733              :          */
    3734            1 :         RelationDropStorage(relation);
    3735              : 
    3736              :         /*
    3737              :          * Create storage for the main fork of the new relfilenode.  If it's a
    3738              :          * table-like object, call into the table AM to do so, which'll also
    3739              :          * create the table's init fork if needed.
    3740              :          *

    3742              :          * caught here, if GetNewRelFileNode messes up for any reason.

    3745            1 :         newrnode.relNode = newrelfilenode;
    3746              : 
    3747            1 :         switch (relation->rd_rel->relkind)
    3748              :         {
    3749            1 :                 case RELKIND_INDEX:
    3750              :                 case RELKIND_SEQUENCE:
    3751              :                         {
    3752              :                                 /* handle these directly, at least for now */
    3753              :                                 SMgrRelation srel;
    3754              : 
    3755            1 :                                 srel = RelationCreateStorage(newrnode, persistence);
    3756            1 :                                 smgrclose(srel);
    3757              :                         }
    3758            1 :                         break;
    3759              : 
    3760            0 :                 case RELKIND_RELATION:
    3761              :                 case RELKIND_TOASTVALUE:
    3762              :                 case RELKIND_MATVIEW:
    3763            0 :                         table_relation_set_new_filenode(relation, &newrnode,
    3764              :                                                                                         persistence,
    3765              :                                                                                         &freezeXid, &minmulti);
    3766            0 :                         break;
    3767              : 
    3768            0 :                 default:
    3769              :                         /* we shouldn't be called for anything else */
    3770            0 :                         elog(ERROR, "relation \"%s\" does not have storage",
    3771              :                                  RelationGetRelationName(relation));
    3772              :                         break;
    3773              :         }
    3774              : 
    3775              :         /*
    3776              :          * If we're dealing with a mapped index, pg_class.relfilenode doesn't
    3777              :          * change; instead we have to send the update to the relation mapper.
    3778              :          *
    3779              :          * For mapped indexes, we don't actually change the pg_class entry at all;
    3780              :          * this is essential when reindexing pg_class itself.  That leaves us with
    3781              :          * possibly-inaccurate values of relpages etc, but those will be fixed up
    3782              :          * later.

    3786              :                 /* This case is only supported for indexes */

    3789              :                 /* Since we're not updating pg_class, these had better not change */

    3791              :                 Assert(classform->relminmxid == minmulti);

    3793              : 
    3794              :                 /*
    3795              :                  * In some code paths it's possible that the tuple update we'd

    3804              :                                                          newrelfilenode,
    3805            0 :                                                          relation->rd_rel->relisshared,
    3806              :                                                          false);
    3807              : 
    3808              :                 /* Since we're not updating pg_class, must trigger inval manually */
    3809            0 :                 CacheInvalidateRelcache(relation);
    3810              :         }
    3811              :         else
    3812              :         {
    3813              :                 /* Normal case, update the pg_class entry */
    3814            1 :                 classform->relfilenode = newrelfilenode;
    3815              : 
    3816              :                 /* relpages etc. never change for sequences */
    3817            1 :                 if (relation->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_SEQUENCE)
    3818              :                 {
    3819            0 :                         classform->relpages = 0;     /* it's empty until further notice */
    3820            0 :                         classform->reltuples = 0;
    3821            0 :                         classform->relallvisible = 0;
    3822              :                 }
    3823            1 :                 classform->relfrozenxid = freezeXid;
    3824            1 :                 classform->relminmxid = minmulti;
    3825            1 :                 classform->relpersistence = persistence;
    3826              : 
    3827            1 :                 CatalogTupleUpdate(pg_class, &otid, tuple);
    3828              :         }
    3829              : 
    3830            1 :         UnlockTuple(pg_class, &otid, InplaceUpdateTupleLock);
    3831            1 :         heap_freetuple(tuple);
    3832              : 
    3833            1 :         table_close(pg_class, RowExclusiveLock);
    3834              : 
    3835              :         /*
    3836              :          * Make the pg_class row change or relation map change visible.  This will
    3837              :          * cause the relcache entry to get updated, too.
    3838              :          */
    3839            1 :         CommandCounterIncrement();
    3840              : 
    3841            1 :         RelationAssumeNewRelfilenode(relation);
    3842            1 : }
    3843              : 
    3844              : /*
    3845              :  * RelationAssumeNewRelfilenode
    3846              :  *
    3847              :  * Code that modifies pg_class.reltablespace or pg_class.relfilenode must call
    3848              :  * this.  The call shall precede any code that might insert WAL records whose
    3849              :  * replay would modify bytes in the new RelFileNode, and the call shall follow
    3850              :  * any WAL modifying bytes in the prior RelFileNode.  See struct RelationData.
    3851              :  * Ideally, call this as near as possible to the CommandCounterIncrement()
    3852              :  * that makes the pg_class change visible (before it or after it); that
    3853              :  * minimizes the chance of future development adding a forbidden WAL insertion
    3854              :  * between RelationAssumeNewRelfilenode() and CommandCounterIncrement().
    3855              :  */
    3856              : void
    3857            4 : RelationAssumeNewRelfilenode(Relation relation)
    3858              : {
    3859            4 :         relation->rd_newRelfilenodeSubid = GetCurrentSubTransactionId();
    3860            4 :         if (relation->rd_firstRelfilenodeSubid == InvalidSubTransactionId)
    3861            4 :                 relation->rd_firstRelfilenodeSubid = relation->rd_newRelfilenodeSubid;
    3862              : 
    3863              :         /* Flag relation as needing eoxact cleanup (to clear these fields) */
    3864            4 :         EOXactListAdd(relation);
    3865            4 : }
    3866              : 
    3867              : 
    3868              : /*
    3869              :  *              RelationCacheInitialize
    3870              :  *
    3871              :  *              This initializes the relation descriptor cache.  At the time
    3872              :  *              that this is invoked, we can't do database access yet (mainly
    3873              :  *              because the transaction subsystem is not up); all we are doing
    3874              :  *              is making an empty cache hashtable.  This must be done before
    3875              :  *              starting the initialization transaction, because otherwise
    3876              :  *              AtEOXact_RelationCache would crash if that transaction aborts
    3877              :  *              before we can get the relcache set up.
    3878              :  */
    3879              : 
    3880              : #define INITRELCACHESIZE                400
    3881              : 
    3882              : void
    3883            8 : RelationCacheInitialize(void)
    3884              : {
    3885              :         HASHCTL         ctl;
    3886              :         int                     allocsize;
    3887              : 
    3888              :         /*
    3889              :          * make sure cache memory context exists
    3890              :          */
    3891            8 :         if (!CacheMemoryContext)
    3892            8 :                 CreateCacheMemoryContext();
    3893              : 
    3894              :         /*
    3895              :          * create hashtable that indexes the relcache
    3896              :          */
    3897          112 :         MemSet(&ctl, 0, sizeof(ctl));
    3898            8 :         ctl.keysize = sizeof(Oid);
    3899            8 :         ctl.entrysize = sizeof(RelIdCacheEnt);
    3900            8 :         RelationIdCache = hash_create("Relcache by OID", INITRELCACHESIZE,
    3901              :                                                                   &ctl, HASH_ELEM | HASH_BLOBS);
    3902              : 
    3903              :         /*
    3904              :          * reserve enough in_progress_list slots for many cases
    3905              :          */
    3906            8 :         allocsize = 4;
    3907            8 :         in_progress_list =
    3908            8 :                 MemoryContextAlloc(CacheMemoryContext,
    3909              :                                                    allocsize * sizeof(*in_progress_list));
    3910            8 :         in_progress_list_maxlen = allocsize;
    3911              : 
    3912              :         /*
    3913              :          * relation mapper needs to be initialized too
    3914              :          */
    3915            8 :         RelationMapInitialize();
    3916            8 : }
    3917              : 
    3918              : /*
    3919              :  *              RelationCacheInitializePhase2
    3920              :  *
    3921              :  *              This is called to prepare for access to shared catalogs during startup.
    3922              :  *              We must at least set up nailed reldescs for pg_database, pg_authid,
    3923              :  *              pg_auth_members, and pg_shseclabel. Ideally we'd like to have reldescs
    3924              :  *              for their indexes, too.  We attempt to load this information from the
    3925              :  *              shared relcache init file.  If that's missing or broken, just make
    3926              :  *              phony entries for the catalogs themselves.
    3927              :  *              RelationCacheInitializePhase3 will clean up as needed.
    3928              :  */
    3929              : void
    3930            8 : RelationCacheInitializePhase2(void)
    3931              : {
    3932              :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
    3933              : 
    3934              :         /*
    3935              :          * relation mapper needs initialized too
    3936              :          */
    3937            8 :         RelationMapInitializePhase2();
    3938              : 
    3939              :         /*
    3940              :          * In bootstrap mode, the shared catalogs aren't there yet anyway, so do
    3941              :          * nothing.
    3942              :          */
    3943            8 :         if (IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
    3944            1 :                 return;
    3945              : 
    3946              :         /*
    3947              :          * switch to cache memory context
    3948              :          */
    3949            7 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
    3950              : 
    3951              :         /*
    3952              :          * Try to load the shared relcache cache file.  If unsuccessful, bootstrap
    3953              :          * the cache with pre-made descriptors for the critical shared catalogs.
    3954              :          */
    3955            7 :         if (!load_relcache_init_file(true))
    3956              :         {
    3957            4 :                 formrdesc("pg_database", DatabaseRelation_Rowtype_Id, true,
    3958              :                                   Natts_pg_database, Desc_pg_database);
    3959            4 :                 formrdesc("pg_authid", AuthIdRelation_Rowtype_Id, true,
    3960              :                                   Natts_pg_authid, Desc_pg_authid);
    3961            4 :                 formrdesc("pg_auth_members", AuthMemRelation_Rowtype_Id, true,
    3962              :                                   Natts_pg_auth_members, Desc_pg_auth_members);
    3963            4 :                 formrdesc("pg_shseclabel", SharedSecLabelRelation_Rowtype_Id, true,
    3964              :                                   Natts_pg_shseclabel, Desc_pg_shseclabel);
    3965            4 :                 formrdesc("pg_subscription", SubscriptionRelation_Rowtype_Id, true,
    3966              :                                   Natts_pg_subscription, Desc_pg_subscription);
    3967              : 
    3968              : #define NUM_CRITICAL_SHARED_RELS        5       /* fix if you change list above */
    3969              :         }
    3970              : 
    3971            7 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
    3972              : }
    3973              : 
    3974              : /*
    3975              :  *              RelationCacheInitializePhase3
    3976              :  *
    3977              :  *              This is called as soon as the catcache and transaction system
    3978              :  *              are functional and we have determined MyDatabaseId.  At this point
    3979              :  *              we can actually read data from the database's system catalogs.
    3980              :  *              We first try to read pre-computed relcache entries from the local
    3981              :  *              relcache init file.  If that's missing or broken, make phony entries
    3982              :  *              for the minimum set of nailed-in-cache relations.  Then (unless
    3983              :  *              bootstrapping) make sure we have entries for the critical system
    3984              :  *              indexes.  Once we've done all this, we have enough infrastructure to

    4607              : /*
    4608              :  * RelationGetIndexList -- get a list of OIDs of indexes on this relation
    4609              :  *
    4610              :  * The index list is created only if someone requests it.  We scan pg_index
    4611              :  * to find relevant indexes, and add the list to the relcache entry so that
    4612              :  * we won't have to compute it again.  Note that shared cache inval of a
    4613              :  * relcache entry will delete the old list and set rd_indexvalid to false,
    4614              :  * so that we must recompute the index list on next request.  This handles
    4615              :  * creation or deletion of an index.
    4616              :  *
    4617              :  * Indexes that are marked not indislive are omitted from the returned list.
    4618              :  * Such indexes are expected to be dropped momentarily, and should not be
    4619              :  * touched at all by any caller of this function.
    4620              :  *
    4621              :  * The returned list is guaranteed to be sorted in order by OID.  This is
    4622              :  * needed by the executor, since for index types that we obtain exclusive
    4623              :  * locks on when updating the index, all backends must lock the indexes in
    4624              :  * the same order or we will get deadlocks (see ExecOpenIndices()).  Any
    4625              :  * consistent ordering would do, but ordering by OID is easy.
    4626              :  *
    4627              :  * Since shared cache inval causes the relcache's copy of the list to go away,
    4628              :  * we return a copy of the list palloc'd in the caller's context.  The caller
    4629              :  * may list_free() the returned list after scanning it. This is necessary
    4630              :  * since the caller will typically be doing syscache lookups on the relevant
    4631              :  * indexes, and syscache lookup could cause SI messages to be processed!
    4632              :  *
    4633              :  * In exactly the same way, we update rd_pkindex, which is the OID of the
    4634              :  * relation's primary key index if any, else InvalidOid; and rd_replidindex,
    4635              :  * which is the pg_class OID of an index to be used as the relation's

    4975              : /*
    4976              :  * RelationGetIndexPredicate -- get the index predicate for an index
    4977              :  *

    4979              :  * node tree (suitable for use in planning).

    4981              :  * Otherwise, the returned tree is copied into the caller's memory context.

    4983              :  * disappear due to relcache invalidation.)

    5044              : /*
    5045              :  * RelationGetIndexAttrBitmap -- get a bitmap of index attribute numbers

    5057              :  * we can include system attributes (e.g., OID) in the bitmap representation.
    5058              :  *

    5609              : /*
    5610              :  * Routines to support ereport() reports of relation-related errors
    5611              :  *
    5612              :  * These could have been put into elog.c, but it seems like a module layering
    5613              :  * violation to have elog.c calling relcache or syscache stuff --- and we

    5615              :  */
    5616              : 
    5617              : /*
    5618              :  * errtable --- stores schema_name and table_name of a table
    5619              :  * within the current errordata.
    5620              :  */

    5622            3 : errtable(Relation rel)
    5623              : {
    5624            3 :         err_generic_string(PG_DIAG_SCHEMA_NAME,
    5625            3 :                                            get_namespace_name(RelationGetNamespace(rel)));
    5626            3 :         err_generic_string(PG_DIAG_TABLE_NAME, RelationGetRelationName(rel));
    5627              : 
    5628            3 :         return 0;                                       /* return value does not matter */
    5629              : }
    5630              : 
    5631              : /*
    5632              :  * errtablecol --- stores schema_name, table_name and column_name
    5633              :  * of a table column within the current errordata.
    5634              :  *

    5636              :  * easier and less error-prone than getting the column name for themselves.
    5637              :  */

    5639            0 : errtablecol(Relation rel, int attnum)

    5649              : 
    5650            0 :         return errtablecolname(rel, colname);
    5651              : }
    5652              : 
    5653              : /*
    5654              :  * errtablecolname --- stores schema_name, table_name and column_name

    5684              : 
    5685              : /*
    5686              :  *      load_relcache_init_file, write_relcache_init_file
    5687              :  *
    5688              :  *              In late 1992, we started regularly having databases with more than
    5689              :  *              a thousand classes in them.  With this number of classes, it became
    5690              :  *              critical to do indexed lookups on the system catalogs.
    5691              :  *
    5692              :  *              Bootstrapping these lookups is very hard.  We want to be able to
    5693              :  *              use an index on pg_attribute, for example, but in order to do so,
    5694              :  *              we must have read pg_attribute for the attributes in the index,
    5695              :  *              which implies that we need to use the index.
    5696              :  *
    5697              :  *              In order to get around the problem, we do the following:
    5698              :  *
    5699              :  *                 +  When the database system is initialized (at initdb time), we
    5700              :  *                        don't use indexes.  We do sequential scans.
    5701              :  *
    5702              :  *                 +  When the backend is started up in normal mode, we load an image
    5703              :  *                        of the appropriate relation descriptors, in internal format,
    5704              :  *                        from an initialization file in the data/base/... directory.
    5705              :  *
    5706              :  *                 +  If the initialization file isn't there, then we create the
    5707              :  *                        relation descriptors using sequential scans and write 'em to
    5708              :  *                        the initialization file for use by subsequent backends.
    5709              :  *
    5710              :  *              As of Postgres 9.0, there is one local initialization file in each
    5711              :  *              database, plus one shared initialization file for shared catalogs.
    5712              :  *
    5713              :  *              We could dispense with the initialization files and just build the
    5714              :  *              critical reldescs the hard way on every backend startup, but that
    5715              :  *              slows down backend startup noticeably.
    5716              :  *
    5717              :  *              We can in fact go further, and save more relcache entries than
    5718              :  *              just the ones that are absolutely critical; this allows us to speed
    5719              :  *              up backend startup by not having to build such entries the hard way.
    5720              :  *              Presently, all the catalog and index entries that are referred to
    5721              :  *              by catcaches are stored in the initialization files.
    5722              :  *
    5723              :  *              The same mechanism that detects when catcache and relcache entries
    5724              :  *              need to be invalidated (due to catalog updates) also arranges to
    5725              :  *              unlink the initialization files when the contents may be out of date.
    5726              :  *              The files will then be rebuilt during the next backend startup.
    5727              :  */
    5728              : 
    5729              : /*
    5730              :  * load_relcache_init_file -- attempt to load cache from the shared
    5731              :  * or local cache init file
    5732              :  *
    5733              :  * If successful, return true and set criticalRelcachesBuilt or
    5734              :  * criticalSharedRelcachesBuilt to true.
    5735              :  * If not successful, return false.
    5736              :  *
    5737              :  * NOTE: we assume we are already switched into CacheMemoryContext.
    5738              :  */

    5747              :                                 max_rels,
    5748              :                                 nailed_rels,
    5749              :                                 nailed_indexes,
    5750              :                                 magic;

    5752              : 
    5753           12 :         if (shared)

    5757            5 :                 snprintf(initfilename, sizeof(initfilename), "%s/%s",

    5760           12 :         fp = AllocateFile(initfilename, PG_BINARY_R);

    5765              :          * Read the index relcache entries from the file.  Note we will not enter

    6371              : 
    6372              : /*
    6373              :  * Determine whether a given relation (identified by OID) is one of the ones
    6374              :  * we should store in a relcache init file.
    6375              :  *
    6376              :  * We must cache all nailed rels, and for efficiency we should cache every rel
    6377              :  * that supports a syscache.  The former set is almost but not quite a subset
    6378              :  * of the latter. The special cases are relations where
    6379              :  * RelationCacheInitializePhase2/3 chooses to nail for efficiency reasons, but
    6380              :  * which do not support any syscache.
    6381              :  */
    6382              : bool
    6383         9053 : RelationIdIsInInitFile(Oid relationId)
    6384              : {
    6385         9053 :         if (relationId == SharedSecLabelRelationId ||
    6386         9048 :                 relationId == TriggerRelidNameIndexId ||
    6387         9048 :                 relationId == DatabaseNameIndexId ||
    6388              :                 relationId == SharedSecLabelObjectIndexId)
    6389              :         {
    6390              :                 /*
    6391              :                  * If this Assert fails, we don't need the applicable special case
    6392              :                  * anymore.
    6393              :                  */
    6394              :                 Assert(!RelationSupportsSysCache(relationId));
    6395            5 :                 return true;
    6396              :         }
    6397         9048 :         return RelationSupportsSysCache(relationId);
    6398              : }
    6399              : 
    6400              : /*
    6401              :  * Invalidate (remove) the init file during commit of a transaction that
    6402              :  * changed one or more of the relation cache entries that are kept in the
    6403              :  * local init file.
    6404              :  *
    6405              :  * To be safe against concurrent inspection or rewriting of the init file,
    6406              :  * we must take RelCacheInitLock, then remove the old init file, then send
    6407              :  * the SI messages that include relcache inval for such relations, and then
    6408              :  * release RelCacheInitLock.  This serializes the whole affair against
    6409              :  * write_relcache_init_file, so that we can be sure that any other process
    6410              :  * that's concurrently trying to create a new init file won't move an
    6411              :  * already-stale version into place after we unlink.  Also, because we unlink
    6412              :  * before sending the SI messages, a backend that's currently starting cannot
    6413              :  * read the now-obsolete init file and then miss the SI messages that will
    6414              :  * force it to update its relcache entries.  (This works because the backend
    6415              :  * startup sequence gets into the sinval array before trying to load the init
    6416              :  * file.)
    6417              :  *
    6418              :  * We take the lock and do the unlink in RelationCacheInitFilePreInvalidate,
    6419              :  * then release the lock in RelationCacheInitFilePostInvalidate.  Caller must
    6420              :  * send any pending SI messages between those calls.

    6427              : 
    6428           71 :         if (DatabasePath)
    6429           71 :                 snprintf(localinitfname, sizeof(localinitfname), "%s/%s",
    6430              :                                  DatabasePath, RELCACHE_INIT_FILENAME);
    6431           71 :         snprintf(sharedinitfname, sizeof(sharedinitfname), "global/%s",
    6432              :                          RELCACHE_INIT_FILENAME);
    6433              : 
    6434           71 :         LWLockAcquire(RelCacheInitLock, LW_EXCLUSIVE);
    6435              : 
    6436              :         /*
    6437              :          * The files might not be there if no backend has been started since the
    6438              :          * last removal.  But complain about failures other than ENOENT with
    6439              :          * ERROR.  Fortunately, it's not too late to abort the transaction if we
    6440              :          * can't get rid of the would-be-obsolete init file.
    6441              :          */
    6442           71 :         if (DatabasePath)

    6445           71 : }
    6446              : 
    6447              : void
    6448           71 : RelationCacheInitFilePostInvalidate(void)
    6449              : {

    6452              : 
    6453              : /*
    6454              :  * Remove the init files during postmaster startup.
    6455              :  *
    6456              :  * We used to keep the init files across restarts, but that is unsafe in PITR
    6457              :  * scenarios, and even in simple crash-recovery cases there are windows for
    6458              :  * the init files to become out-of-sync with the database.  So now we just
    6459              :  * remove them during startup and expect the first backend launch to rebuild
    6460              :  * them.  Of course, this has to happen in each database of the cluster.
    6461              :  */

    6463            3 : RelationCacheInitFileRemove(void)

    6469              : 
    6470            3 :         snprintf(path, sizeof(path), "global/%s",
    6471              :                          RELCACHE_INIT_FILENAME);
    6472            3 :         unlink_initfile(path, LOG);
    6473              : 
    6474              :         /* Scan everything in the default tablespace */
    6475            3 :         RelationCacheInitFileRemoveInDir("base");
    6476              : 
    6477              :         /* Scan the tablespace link directory to find non-default tablespaces */
    6478            3 :         dir = AllocateDir(tblspcdir);
    6479              : 
    6480           12 :         while ((de = ReadDirExtended(dir, tblspcdir, LOG)) != NULL)
    6481              :         {
    6482            6 :                 if (strspn(de->d_name, "0123456789") == strlen(de->d_name))
    6483              :                 {
    6484              :                         /* Scan the tablespace dir for per-database dirs */
    6485            0 :                         snprintf(path, sizeof(path), "%s/%s/%s",
    6486            0 :                                          tblspcdir, de->d_name, TABLESPACE_VERSION_DIRECTORY);
    6487            0 :                         RelationCacheInitFileRemoveInDir(path);
    6488              :                 }
    6489              :         }
    6490              : 
    6491            3 :         FreeDir(dir);
    6492            3 : }

    6494              : /* Process one per-tablespace directory for RelationCacheInitFileRemove */
    6495              : static void
    6496            3 : RelationCacheInitFileRemoveInDir(const char *tblspcpath)
    6497              : {

    6501              :

    6503            3 :         dir = AllocateDir(tblspcpath);

    6508              :                 {
    6509              :                         /* Try to remove the init file in each database */
    6510            5 :                         snprintf(initfilename, sizeof(initfilename), "%s/%s/%s",
    6511            5 :                                          tblspcpath, de->d_name, RELCACHE_INIT_FILENAME);

    6521              : {
